subject: Statistics
title: How Odds Work
description: A quick, practical guide to How Odds Work.

hook.text: A team is listed at +150. What does that mean? It's a 40% implied probability, hidden in a number.

buildup.text: American odds show profit on a $100 bet. +150 means win $150 on $100. -150 means bet $150 to win $100.

discovery.text: Convert to implied probability: for +150, it's 100/(100+150) = 40%. The bookmaker thinks this team wins 40%.

twist.text: The probabilities for all outcomes add up to more than 100%. The extra is the bookmaker's cut — the vigorish.

climax.text: Understanding odds means understanding implied probability. The bookmaker's opinion is embedded in every line.

punchline.text: Odds are probabilities in disguise. Learn to read them.

quiz.question: Why do implied probabilities from betting odds add up to more than 100%?
options: The extra percentage is the bookmaker's profit margin — called the vigorish or 'vig'
